revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11469988]] Wikidata name: Yamatsuteru-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yamatsuteru Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yamatuteru jinja honden.JPG |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]] Notose 390 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.330472|N|136.322611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Main_Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-Bay Style) |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Annual_Festival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] |Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Maihara|WD=Q824020}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine''' (山津照神社, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a listed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with its former shrine Rank classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine's crest (''kamon'') features chrysanthemums and paulownias. == deity == * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto]] == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. It is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a listed shrine (''shikinai-sha'') and is said to enshrine a divided spirit from {{ill|Hoyama Shrine|ja|帆山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Historical records state that the shrine was awarded the sixth Rank (''Juroku-i'') in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851 CE) and promoted to fourth Rank (''Jushi-i'') in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 8 (866 CE). In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-2.jpg|Honden File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Chūmon (middle gate) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Shiga Prefectural Historic Site) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii == Associated Shrines == * {{ill|Aoki Shrine|ja|青木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|若宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=春日大社}} * {{ill|Kitano Shrine|ja|北野社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=北野天満宮|1=Kitano Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q662176}} == Annual Rituals == * festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1050 Yamatsuteru Shrine - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社|version=104506930|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Omi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:山津照神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] {{Commons category|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11469988}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Main_Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-Bay Style) |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Annual_Festival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] |Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Maihara|WD=Q824020}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine''' (山津照神社, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a listed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with its former shrine Rank classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine's crest (''kamon'') features chrysanthemums and paulownias. == deity == * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto]] == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. It is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a listed shrine (''shikinai-sha'') and is said to enshrine a divided spirit from {{ill|Hoyama Shrine|ja|帆山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Historical records state that the shrine was awarded the sixth Rank (''Juroku-i'') in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851 CE) and promoted to fourth Rank (''Jushi-i'') in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 8 (866 CE). In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Main_Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-Bay Style) |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Annual_Festival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] |Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Maihara|WD=Q824020}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine''' (山津照神社, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a listed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with its former shrine Rank classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine's crest (''kamon'') features chrysanthemums and paulownias. == deity == * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto]] == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. It is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a listed shrine (''shikinai-sha'') and is said to enshrine a divided spirit from {{ill|Hoyama Shrine|ja|帆山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Historical records state that the shrine was awarded the sixth Rank (''Juroku-i'') in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851 CE) and promoted to fourth Rank (''Jushi-i'') in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 8 (866 CE). In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Main_Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-Bay Style) |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Annual_Festival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] |Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Maihara|WD=Q824020}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine''' (山津照神社, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a listed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with its former shrine Rank classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine's crest (''kamon'') features chrysanthemums and paulownias. == deity == * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto]] == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. It is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a listed shrine (''shikinai-sha'') and is said to enshrine a divided spirit from {{ill|Hoyama Shrine|ja|帆山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Historical records state that the shrine was awarded the sixth Rank (''Juroku-i'') in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851 CE) and promoted to fourth Rank (''Jushi-i'') in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 8 (866 CE). In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Main_Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-Bay Style) |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Annual_Festival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] |Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Maihara|WD=Q824020}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine''' (山津照神社, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a listed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with its former shrine Rank classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine's crest (''kamon'') features chrysanthemums and paulownias. == deity == * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto]] == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. It is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a listed shrine (''shikinai-sha'') and is said to enshrine a divided spirit from {{ill|Hoyama Shrine|ja|帆山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Historical records state that the shrine was awarded the sixth Rank (''Juroku-i'') in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851 CE) and promoted to fourth Rank (''Jushi-i'') in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 8 (866 CE). In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. [[sn:commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11469988]] Wikidata name: Yamatsuteru-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yamatsuteru Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yamatuteru jinja honden.JPG |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]] Notose 390 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.330472|N|136.322611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Main_Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-Bay Style) |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Annual_Festival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] |Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Maihara|WD=Q824020}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine''' (山津照神社, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a listed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with its former shrine Rank classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine's crest (''kamon'') features chrysanthemums and paulownias. == deity == * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto]] == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. It is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a listed shrine (''shikinai-sha'') and is said to enshrine a divided spirit from {{ill|Hoyama Shrine|ja|帆山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Historical records state that the shrine was awarded the sixth Rank (''Juroku-i'') in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851 CE) and promoted to fourth Rank (''Jushi-i'') in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 8 (866 CE). In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-2.jpg|Honden File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Chūmon (middle gate) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Shiga Prefectural Historic Site) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii == Associated Shrines == * {{ill|Aoki Shrine|ja|青木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|若宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=春日大社}} * {{ill|Kitano Shrine|ja|北野社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=北野天満宮|1=Kitano Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q662176}} == Annual Rituals == * festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1050 Yamatsuteru Shrine - revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11469988]] Wikidata name: Yamatsuteru-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yamatsuteru Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yamatuteru jinja honden.JPG |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]] Notose 390 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.330472|N|136.322611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Main_Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-Bay Style) |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Annual_Festival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] |Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Maihara|WD=Q824020}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine''' (山津照神社, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a listed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with its former shrine Rank classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine's crest (''kamon'') features chrysanthemums and paulownias. == deity == * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto]] == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. It is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a listed shrine (''shikinai-sha'') and is said to enshrine a divided spirit from {{ill|Hoyama Shrine|ja|帆山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Historical records state that the shrine was awarded the sixth Rank (''Juroku-i'') in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851 CE) and promoted to fourth Rank (''Jushi-i'') in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 8 (866 CE). In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-2.jpg|Honden File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Chūmon (middle gate) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Shiga Prefectural Historic Site) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii == Associated Shrines == * {{ill|Aoki Shrine|ja|青木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|若宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=春日大社}} * {{ill|Kitano Shrine|ja|北野社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=北野天満宮|1=Kitano Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q662176}} == Annual Rituals == * festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1050 Yamatsuteru Shrine - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社|version=104506930|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Omi Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:山津照神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] {{Commons category|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11469988}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{wikidata link|Q11469988}} [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]]

[[commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11469988]] Wikidata name: Yamatsuteru-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yamatsuteru Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yamatuteru jinja honden.JPG |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]] Notose 390 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.330472|N|136.322611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Main_Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-Bay Style) |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Annual_Festival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] |Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Maihara|WD=Q824020}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine''' (山津照神社, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a listed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with its former shrine Rank classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine's crest (''kamon'') features chrysanthemums and paulownias. == deity == * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto]] == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. It is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a listed shrine (''shikinai-sha'') and is said to enshrine a divided spirit from {{ill|Hoyama Shrine|ja|帆山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Historical records state that the shrine was awarded the sixth Rank (''Juroku-i'') in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851 CE) and promoted to fourth Rank (''Jushi-i'') in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 8 (866 CE). In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. [[commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] [[commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11469988]] Wikidata name: Yamatsuteru-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yamatsuteru Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yamatuteru jinja honden.JPG |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]] Notose 390 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.330472|N|136.322611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Main_Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-Bay Style) |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Annual_Festival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] |Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Maihara|WD=Q824020}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine''' (山津照神社, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a listed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with its former shrine Rank classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine's crest (''kamon'') features chrysanthemums and paulownias. == deity == * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto]] == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. It is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a listed shrine (''shikinai-sha'') and is said to enshrine a divided spirit from {{ill|Hoyama Shrine|ja|帆山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Historical records state that the shrine was awarded the sixth Rank (''Juroku-i'') in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851 CE) and promoted to fourth Rank (''Jushi-i'') in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 8 (866 CE). In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-2.jpg|Honden File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Chūmon (middle gate) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Shiga Prefectural Historic Site) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii == Associated Shrines == * {{ill|Aoki Shrine|ja|青木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|若宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=春日大社}} * {{ill|Kitano Shrine|ja|北野社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=北野天満宮|1=Kitano Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q662176}} == Annual Rituals == * festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1050 Yamatsuteru Shrine - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社|version=104506930|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:山津照神社]] {{Commons category|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11469988}} [[Category:Buildings in Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Omi Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] [[commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11469988]] Wikidata name: Yamatsuteru-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yamatsuteru Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yamatuteru jinja honden.JPG |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]] Notose 390 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.330472|N|136.322611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Main_Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-Bay Style) |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Annual_Festival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] |Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Maihara|WD=Q824020}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine''' (山津照神社, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a listed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with its former shrine Rank classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine's crest (''kamon'') features chrysanthemums and paulownias. == deity == * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto]] == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. It is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a listed shrine (''shikinai-sha'') and is said to enshrine a divided spirit from {{ill|Hoyama Shrine|ja|帆山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Historical records state that the shrine was awarded the sixth Rank (''Juroku-i'') in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851 CE) and promoted to fourth Rank (''Jushi-i'') in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 8 (866 CE). In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-2.jpg|Honden File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Chūmon (middle gate) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Shiga Prefectural Historic Site) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii == Associated Shrines == * {{ill|Aoki Shrine|ja|青木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|若宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=春日大社}} * {{ill|Kitano Shrine|ja|北野社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=北野天満宮|1=Kitano Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q662176}} == Annual Rituals == * festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1050 Yamatsuteru Shrine - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社|version=104506930|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山津照神社]] {{Commons category|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11469988}} [[Category:Buildings in Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Omi Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-2.jpg|Honden File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Chūmon (middle gate) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Shiga Prefectural Historic Site) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii == Associated Shrines == * {{ill|Aoki Shrine|ja|青木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|若宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=春日大社}} * {{ill|Kitano Shrine|ja|北野社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=北野天満宮|1=Kitano Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q662176}} == Annual Rituals == * festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1050 Yamatsuteru Shrine - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社|version=104506930|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山津照神社]] {{Commons category|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11469988}} [[Category:Buildings in Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Omi Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11469988]] Wikidata name: Yamatsuteru-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yamatsuteru Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yamatuteru jinja honden.JPG |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]] Notose 390 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.330472|N|136.322611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuni-no-Tokotachi]] |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Main_Hall = [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|Nagare-zukuri]] (Three-Bay Style) |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Annual_Festival = [[:en:May 5|May 5]] |Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Maihara|WD=Q824020}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine''' (山津照神社, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Maibara, Shiga|Maibara City]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a listed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with its former shrine Rank classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine's crest (''kamon'') features chrysanthemums and paulownias. == deity == * [[:en:Kuni-no-Tokotachi|Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto]] == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. It is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a listed shrine (''shikinai-sha'') and is said to enshrine a divided spirit from {{ill|Hoyama Shrine|ja|帆山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Echizen Province|Echizen Province]]. Historical records state that the shrine was awarded the sixth Rank (''Juroku-i'') in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851 CE) and promoted to fourth Rank (''Jushi-i'') in [[:en:Zhenguan|Jogan]] 8 (866 CE). In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-2.jpg|Honden File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Chūmon (middle gate) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Shiga Prefectural Historic Site) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii == Associated Shrines == * {{ill|Aoki Shrine|ja|青木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|若宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=春日大社}} * {{ill|Kitano Shrine|ja|北野社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=北野天満宮|1=Kitano Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q662176}} == Annual Rituals == * festival ([[:en:May 5|May 5]]) == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1050 Yamatsuteru Shrine - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社|version=104506930|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山津照神社]] {{Commons category|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11469988}} [[Category:Buildings in Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Omi Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Yamatsuteru-jinja]][[ja:山津照神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11469988]] Wikidata name: Yamatsuteru-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Yamatsuteru Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yamatuteru jinja honden.JPG |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}, {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara City}} Notose 390 |coordinates = {{Coord|35.330472|N|136.322611|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity = {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} |Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor)
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Main_Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} (Three-Bay Style) |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Annual_Festival = {{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5}} |Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Maihara|WD=Q824020}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine''' (山津照神社, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara City}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a listed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with its former shrine Rank classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine's crest (''kamon'') features chrysanthemums and paulownias. == deity == * {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto}} == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. It is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a listed shrine (''shikinai-sha'') and is said to enshrine a divided spirit from {{ill|Hoyama Shrine|ja|帆山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province}}. Historical records state that the shrine was awarded the sixth Rank (''Juroku-i'') in {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju}} 1 (851 CE) and promoted to fourth Rank (''Jushi-i'') in {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jogan}} 8 (866 CE). In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-2.jpg|Honden File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Chūmon (middle gate) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Shiga Prefectural Historic Site) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii == Associated Shrines == * {{ill|Aoki Shrine|ja|青木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|若宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=春日大社}} * {{ill|Kitano Shrine|ja|北野社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=北野天満宮|1=Kitano Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q662176}} == Annual Rituals == * festival ({{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5}}) == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1050 Yamatsuteru Shrine - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社|version=104506930|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山津照神社]] {{Commons category|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11469988}} [[Category:Buildings in Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Omi Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Main_Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri}} (Three-Bay Style) |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Annual_Festival = {{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5}} |Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Maihara|WD=Q824020}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine''' (山津照神社, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara City}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a listed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with its former shrine Rank classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine's crest (''kamon'') features chrysanthemums and paulownias. == deity == * {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto}} == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. It is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a listed shrine (''shikinai-sha'') and is said to enshrine a divided spirit from {{ill|Hoyama Shrine|ja|帆山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province}}. Historical records state that the shrine was awarded the sixth Rank (''Juroku-i'') in {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju}} 1 (851 CE) and promoted to fourth Rank (''Jushi-i'') in {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jogan}} 8 (866 CE). In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. It is a listed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with its former shrine Rank classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. Historical records state that the shrine was awarded the sixth Rank (''Juroku-i'') in {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju}} 1 (851 CE) and promoted to fourth Rank (''Jushi-i'') in {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jogan}} 8 (866 CE). In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-2.jpg|Honden File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Chūmon (middle gate) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Shiga Prefectural Historic Site) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii == Associated Shrines == * {{ill|Aoki Shrine|ja|青木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|若宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=春日大社}} * {{ill|Kitano Shrine|ja|北野社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=北野天満宮|1=Kitano Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q662176}} == Annual Rituals == * festival ({{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5}}) == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1050 Yamatsuteru Shrine - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社|version=104506930|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山津照神社]] {{Commons category|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11469988}} [[Category:Buildings in Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Omi Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It is a listed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with its former shrine Rank classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine's crest (''kamon'') features chrysanthemums and paulownias. == deity == * {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. It is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a listed shrine (''shikinai-sha'') and is said to enshrine a divided spirit from {{ill|Hoyama Shrine|ja|帆山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}}. Historical records state that the shrine was awarded the sixth Rank (''Juroku-i'') in {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju|1=Ninju}} 1 (851 CE) and promoted to fourth Rank (''Jushi-i'') in {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jogan|1=Zhenguan}} 8 (866 CE). In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-2.jpg|Honden File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, honden-1.jpg|Chūmon (middle gate) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|{{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Shiga Prefectural Historic Site) File:Yamatsuteru-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii == Associated Shrines == * {{ill|Aoki Shrine|ja|青木神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoki Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|若宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga-taisha|lt=Kasuga Shrine|ja|春日社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=春日大社}} * {{ill|Kitano Shrine|ja|北野社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitano Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=北野天満宮|1=Kitano Tenmangū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q662176}} == Annual Rituals == * festival ({{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5|1=May 5}}) == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1050 Yamatsuteru Shrine - Shiga Prefecture Shrine Office] {{Shinto horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|山津照神社|version=104506930|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:山津照神社]] {{Commons category|Yamatsuteru-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11469988}} [[Category:Buildings in Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Maibara, Shiga]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Omi Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site. Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Main_Hall = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} (Three-Bay Style) |Alias = |Pilgrimage = |Annual_Festival = {{ill|May 5|en|May 5|WD=Q2550|lt=May 5|1=May 5}} |Rituals = |prefecture= {{ill|Maihara|WD=Q824020|1=Maibara, Shiga}} |architecture_style= {{ill|nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} }} '''Yamatsuteru Shrine''' (山津照神社, ''Yamatsuteru-jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|WD=Q824020|lt=Maibara City|1=Maibara, Shiga}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a listed {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, with its former shrine Rank classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. The shrine's crest (''kamon'') features chrysanthemums and paulownias. == deity == * {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|WD=Q478504|lt=Kuninotokotachi no Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} == History == The shrine's founding date is unknown. It is recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as a listed shrine (''shikinai-sha'') and is said to enshrine a divided spirit from {{ill|Hoyama Shrine|ja|帆山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hoyama Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|WD=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}}. Historical records state that the shrine was awarded the sixth Rank (''Juroku-i'') in {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|WD=Q1373077|lt=Ninju|1=Ninju}} 1 (851 CE) and promoted to fourth Rank (''Jushi-i'') in {{ill|Zhenguan|en|Zhenguan|WD=Q3577278|lt=Jogan|1=Zhenguan}} 8 (866 CE). In the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, the shrine was designated a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1881. It was later promoted to a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1921. == Grounds == The shrine precincts include the {{ill|Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|ja|山津照神社古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatsuteru Shrine Kofun|qq=}}, an ancient Kofun designated as a Shiga Prefectural Historic Site.
